assertionResults.title: BETTER brand dominates above the fold
assertionResults.reason: The first viewport is unmistakably BETTER-led: the hero logotype is the largest visual brand element above the fold, while the explanatory copy, status chips, and CTAs remain visually subordinate.

observations: At 1440px desktop width, the BETTER logotype sits centrally in the hero and reads as the loudest brand signal before any scroll.
The header also carries the same BETTER asset, but it remains smaller than the hero lockup, reinforcing a single dominant above-the-fold brand statement rather than competing with it.
evidence.consoleErrors: none (only development-mode React DevTools/HMR informational logs)

assertionResults.title: Signature BETTER shell matches the approved overhaul
assertionResults.reason: The shipped experience preserves the approved shell overhaul: a poster-like BETTER hero, graph-first atlas entry, premium near-black/electric-blue tradebetter palette, and persistent Radiant/Hermes atmosphere instead of a generic dashboard treatment.

observations: The hero reads as one immersive BETTER composition with a dominant logotype, restrained copy stack, and electric-blue atmospheric depth behind the content.
After entering the atlas and moving through roadmap/architecture surfaces, the shell keeps the same graph-first, tradebetter-led visual language rather than switching into a disconnected page style.
evidence.consoleErrors: none (only development-mode React DevTools/HMR informational logs)

assertionResults.title: Provided BETTER logotype asset replaces text wordmarks across key shell surfaces
assertionResults.reason: The shipped shell uses the provided BETTER SVG asset for the hero, header, and mobile overlay instead of rendering BETTER as a plain text wordmark on those surfaces.

observations: Browser inspection shows the hero and header using the same BETTER lockup asset in the live shell.
Code trace confirms the shared BetterLogotype component is wired into the header, hero, and mobile navigation overlay surfaces.

assertionResults.title: Radiant/Hermes atmosphere remains materially visible across the full shell
assertionResults.reason: The real Radiant-derived shader field and Hermes-derived ASCII texture remain visibly present not only in the hero, but also through roadmap at-rest, focused graph detail, and lower-page exploration states.

observations: The hero shows broad electric-blue shader depth and terminal-like ASCII texture as substantive background structure rather than a flat gradient wash.

Roadmap and architecture surfaces still show the same atmospheric treatment at reduced strength, confirming the immersive language continues below the hero instead of disappearing after the first section.

assertionResults.title: tradebetter theme parity extends across the full shell
assertionResults.reason: The shell keeps tradebetter parity across hero, atlas, roadmap, and architecture surfaces by consistently using the near-black foundation, electric-blue accents, and IBM Plex Mono terminal typography.

observations: Across all captured states, the shell remains anchored in near-black surfaces with electric-blue emphasis and mono terminal framing rather than reverting to a neutral/light page treatment.

Theme tokens in code explicitly map to tradebetter values: #0a0a0c for background, #455eff for accent emphasis, IBM Plex Mono for UI/terminal typography, and blue remaps in both shader and ASCII systems.

assertionResults.title: Persistent atmosphere preserves readability and interaction clarity
assertionResults.reason: Despite the immersive background, copy, CTAs, roadmap labels, and lower-page content remain clearly readable and usable across every tested shell state.

observations: Hero copy, status chips, and CTA buttons remain crisp on top of the shader/ASCII stack, with no atmospheric layer obscuring the primary narrative block.

Roadmap tabs, graph detail content, and lower-page architecture copy remain readable against the persistent background, indicating the reduced-opacity continuation is correctly balanced for legibility.

assertionResults.title: BETTER logotype usage is traceable to the provided asset
assertionResults.reason: The shipped BETTER logotype is directly traceable to the provided Better_Design source asset: the repo SVG and the supplied source SVG are SHA-256 identical, and the BetterLogotype component documents and serves that exact asset path across the shell.

observations: `public/better-logotype.svg` and `/Users/test/Downloads/Better_Design/Logo/Better_Logotype_Light.svg` produced the same SHA-256 digest during validation, confirming byte-identical provenance.
The BetterLogotype component comment explicitly names the provided source asset, and the live hero/header/mobile surfaces all consume `/better-logotype.svg`.

summary.note: All 7 assigned shell-theme assertions passed in the headed browser run. The live shell keeps the BETTER logotype dominant above the fold, extends tradebetter theme parity and Radiant/Hermes atmosphere across graph-first and lower-page surfaces, preserves readability under the persistent background, and serves a logotype asset that is byte-identical to the provided Better_Design source file. No browser runtime errors were observed beyond normal development-mode informational logs.
